videos.title: YARNS @ JAZZEXZESS / House of Music | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
The Yarns Ensemble is a collective initiated by drummer and producer Lukas Akintaya (Adeolu). Approaching sound as a spiritual and communal practice – through flexible song cells and improvisation, they meld memory, rhythm, and resonance into an intense experience of ritual, healing, and belonging.

videos.title: ELIAS STEMESEDER TRIO – Perspectives | Part 3 | Konzerthaus Berlin
videos.description:
Based on transcriptions and analyses of antiphonal songs, dance forms, and instrumental pieces, the Elias Stemeseder Trio creates a proto-folkloric cosmos. Cultural artifacts—seen in a new light and transcribed through the prism of contemporary understanding—form the cornerstones of a program that reflects musical archetypes that span time and region. Structural rigor and simple vocality, virtuoso precision and mantric circling, compositional diversity and spontaneous arrangement, condense into a musical language whose questions are negotiated in improvisational discourse by an ensemble trained in diverse disciplines—jazz, drone, old and new music, and Afro-Cuban batá.

ELIAS STEMESEDER TRIO live @ Konzerthaus, Berlin | 2026
STOP OVER 4 – Perspectives (Part 3)

As part of the pilot phase known as “Zentrum Under Construction”, the “STOP OVER” event series has been exploring key aspects of the future institution „House of Jazz – a center for jazz and improvised music“ since 2023. The fourth stopover on the path to establishing the „Zentrum“ focused on curation: Three internationally renowned musicians have been invited to shape one evening each within this three-day series. Each artist brought their own perspective and musical experience, opening up fresh viewpoints and new spaces of sound.

Part 1 – curated by Ingrid Laubrock
﻿SET 1 DAVIES-MARINO-WALKER
SET 2 ﻿YUHAN SU: NUANCE
SET 3 ﻿FRED MOTEN & BRANDON LÓPEZ

Part 2 – curated by Julia Hülsmann

SET 2 ﻿LORENZO SOULÈS & ENSEMBLE APPARAT

videos.title: SO SORRY @ Club Gretchen | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
The music of SO SORRY moves at the intersection of contemporary jazz, alternative pop and R&B, delivering intricate melodies and tightly woven arrangements with emotional vulnerability and an ethos of shared empowerment.
With consummate musicianship and heartfelt songwriting at its core, SO SORRY is not an apology but a statement of reclaiming space and embracing boldness.
SO SORRY share a space of energy and vulnerability with their audience and celebrate the diversity of their different musical backgrounds blending them into a powerful unified sound.

videos.title: BEATDENKER @ PANKE | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
"A mind-bending experience and fascinating electronic voyage. The energetic Beats with their improvisational magic are arriving from Jazz's spontaneity. Music truely has no boundaries and Beatdenker's music stands as a pleasant narrative for it." - Illustrate Magazine (New York City)​

"We’re always on the hunt for artists doing the unexpected – artists who break boundaries and do things that nobody else would think to do. And, today, we’ve got maybe the most out-of-bounds thing we’ve ever covered, courtesy of German, one-of-a-kind, creative spirit and technically skilled artist BEATDENKER." - Magnetic Magazine (Los Angeles)

"Beatdenker is a meta artist! Beatdenker's art is wormhole to a postcontemporary era of music." - SinusOidalMusic (Bangalore)

videos.title: YUHAN SU - NUANCE  – Perspectives | Part 1 | Konzerthaus Berlin
videos.description:
Vibraphonist and composer Yuhan Su takes the stage in a special trio edition, featuring Michaël Attias on saxophone and Shinya Lin on piano along with live electronics. Together, they present music from Su’s 2025 album OVER the MOONs, as well as a series of new works, including the world premiere of Symphony Behind the Hills. 

At the center is Su’s vibraphone—a meeting point of melodies, harmonies and rhythms, functioning as a painter of sonic landscapes. Her music is shaped by memories and language, drawing from her upbringing in Taiwan and her fluency in Mandarin. Shinya Lin brings a distinct sonic approach, combining self-developed electronic instruments, prepared piano, and free improvisation. Michaël Attias adds his deeply collaborative and inquisitive voice on saxophone. Navigating the spaces between composition and improvisation, the trio explores counterpoint, timbre, and rhythmic gravity, crafting pulsing soundscapes full of surprise and color. 

YUHAN SU - NUANCE live @ Konzerthaus, Berlin | 2026
STOP OVER 4 – Perspectives (Part 1)

As part of the pilot phase known as “Zentrum Under Construction”, the “STOP OVER” event series has been exploring key aspects of the future institution „House of Jazz – a center for jazz and improvised music“ since 2023. The fourth stopover on the path to establishing the „Zentrum“ focused on curation: Three internationally renowned musicians have been invited to shape one evening each within this three-day series. videos.title: KATHRIN PECHLOF TRIO - live @ Musikbrauerei, Berlin | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
At the intersection of improvised music, contemporary jazz, and post-genre creative music, the KATHRIN PECHLOF TRIO unfolds a finely nuanced chamber music–like sound world. Here, biographically informed musical material merges with influences from Impressionism and classical modernism, as well as with practices of free improvisation and contrapunctal compositional techniques. The boundaries between composition and improvisation become deliberately fluid, and traditional roles dissolve: each member of the trio is at once soloist, accompanist, instigator, and guide. In this way, a shared sonic language emerges in which the ensemble itself becomes a “meta-instrument” — a collective, breathing entity.

KATHRIN PECHLOF TRIO - live @ Musikbrauerei, Berlin | 2026
FILMED BY EAR is a series by @berta.berlin.

Kathrin Pechlof – harp + composition

videos.title: SATISFACTIONACTION @ Deutsche Oper, Berlin | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
In SATISFACTIONACTION the gratification is both felt on an individual level and celebrated communally, with the public immersed in a multisensory experience. It also poses the question: can a transparently escapist, communal moment counterbalance a permanent feeling of crisis and pressure? Does communal satisfaction provide the basis for agreeing on a new understanding of reality, or is it just us running away from a world on fire?

The contemporary music of composer Max Andrzejewski is informed by the minimal and jazz genres and the ASMR movement. As a percussionist with improvised-music leanings, he is also one of the eight members of the SATISFACTIONACTION ensemble, which is made up of Kaleidoskop soloists and other musicians with close artistic links to Andrzejewski. This collaboration with the singers of the Vocantare Chorus and choreographer Sylvana Seddig is embedded within the space and video installation of Lukas Zerbst. The result is a composition consisting of spatially and temporally shifting and overlapping layers, merging into constellations, one that mirrors and renders experiential the bases of our need for fulfillment.

Impressions of the installation music theatre piece SATISFACTIONACTION by Max Andrzejewski - premiered in 2025. A co-production by Deutsche Oper Berlin, Beethovenfest Bonn and Max Andrzejewski - supported by Hauptstadtkulturfonds.

SATISFACTIONACTION @ Deutsche Oper, Berlin | 2025
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: DAVIES MARINO WALKER – Perspectives | Part 1 | Konzerthaus Berlin
videos.description:
Davies-Marino-Walker is a new trio grounded in a shared commitment to active listening, experimentation, and expanding the sonic possibilities of their instruments. Angharad Davies brings her unique vocabulary of alternative resonating bodies, while Jessie Marino infuses abstract ideas drawn from popular culture and the everyday. Deborah Walker, in turn, explores the intersection between music and other art forms.
Folk traditions have long influenced their individual practices, leading them to explore the interconnectedness of music and the art of weaving, a vital craft found worldwide that has remained largely unchanged throughout history. Weaved objects shape everyday life in often overlooked yet profound ways. The trio’s Ecstatic Looms pieces, inspired by historic weaving
techniques, use notation from traditional pattern books as a springboard for improvised musical structures and materials, weaving together sound and texture—always welcoming accidental discoveries along the way.

DAVIES MARINO WALKER live @ Konzerthaus, Berlin | 2026

videos.title: CLARA HABERKAMP TRIO live @ JazzHall Hamburg
videos.description:
"Clara Haberkamp is making waves on the German jazz scene, featuring Norwegian drummer Jarle Vespestad alongside Eva Kruse on bass. The set fizzes with energy,  invention and emotion.  The material is a mixture of Haberkamp’s own writing and some intriguing choices.
Tightly woven patterns become subtly different with repetition and gradually break up, piano and bass seeming to float around each other, jostled by cross-rhythms from Vespestad’s drums.
The resulting music is, in the spirit of great trios, an absorbing conversation between top-drawer musicians. "

CLARA HABERKAMP TRIO - live @ JazzHall Hamburg | 2026

videos.title: FRED MOTEN & BRANDON LOPEZ – Perspectives | Part 1 | Konzerthaus Berlin
videos.description:
Fred Moten and Brandon López make incendiary music for a burning world. Their infamous live 
shows explore a propulsive, relentless, and energetic large-scale developing form. Poet and cultural 
theorist Fred Moten creates new conceptual spaces that accommodate emergent forms of Black cultural production, aesthetics, and social life. The composer  and bassist Brandon López engages with work,  improvisation, and finding new modalities and sonic possibilities on the double bass. 
As a duo, they concern themselves with the necessity for improvisation of navigating the ascending reign of long-institutionalized madness while simultaneously keeping humanity and sanity intact. In 2025, their synergistic mesh in duo was presented on record for the first time on Revision. 

FRED MOTEN & BRANDON LOPEZ live @ Konzerthaus, Berlin | 2026

videos.title: KOKO TRIO @ STOP OVER 4 – Perspectives | Part 2 | Konzerthaus Berlin
videos.description:
The KOKO Trio brings together three outstanding and multi-award-winning musicians. Taiko Saito, Niko Meinhold, and Moritz Baumgärtner create a 
musical space in which jazz, contemporary music, and free improvisation blend together to become what seems to be the most natural thing in the world.
The marimba is a rather rare instrument in jazz. With it, Taiko Saito creates spherical soundscapes across five and a half octaves. Niko Meinhold contributes impressionistic depth and an experimental touch on the piano. With innovation and dynamism, drummer Moritz Baumgärtner adds another dimension of improvisation to the playing of Saito and Meinhold, who have been a team for over 20 years. In their interplay, the trio successfully blurs the boundaries between jazz, new music, Japanese influences, and punk to create sound structures that strike just the right balance between introspection and energetic outbursts.

KOKO TRIO live @ Konzerthaus, Berlin | 2026
STOP OVER 4 – Perspectives (Part 2)

videos.title: GOŁOS X JASINSKA @ STOP OVER 4 – Perspectives | Part 2 | Konzerthaus Berlin
videos.description:
Gołos x Jasinska is a musical collaboration between pianist Emilia Gołos and vocalist Zuza Jasinska, rooted in friendship and a shared artistic vision. Together, they create music that is intimate, fragile, and minimal, yet strikingly expressive, with a strong emphasis on emotional depth. They combine experimental approaches with song-based forms, blending the acoustic with the electronic and the tonal with the atonal. Gołos’ contemporary piano techniques of improvisation meet Jasinska’s voice, navigating between jazz, folk, singersongwriter, and experimental music. There is a certain patience in how sounds sustain and decay, vibrations overlap, and micro dynamics shift—creating a fragile equilibrium inviting an immersive and intimate listening experience.

GOŁOS X JASINSKA live @ Konzerthaus, Berlin | 2026

videos.title: KAUFMANN DUNSTON PORTUGAL @ Bonello Studio | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
"One of the demands of the improvising musician is to expose themselves to new musical situations. Berlin offers a lot of opportunities for this thanks to the constant influx and
continuous movement of the scene. What we bring to the process as individuals flows into the collectively created music - our personalities, stories, influences. Nick Dunston and Mariá
Portugal are groundbreaking on their instruments and as composers and conceptualists. Both are contemporary artists, they change what already exists. But they are also connected to rich musical traditions. For me, this mixture is inspiring and promising. I am looking forward to creating music in which sound, kinetic energy and provocation all have their place."
(Achim Kaufmann, August 2023)

KAUFMANN / DUNSTON / PORTUGAL  @ Bonello Studio, Berlin | 2025
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: TYSHAWN SOREY TRIO live @ Stadtgarten, Köln | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2025
videos.description:
"Tyshawn Sorey is dedicated to ‘reformulating perceptions of modern Black/Afrodiasporic creative practice.’ With roots in the complexities of avant-garde classical, he was honoured with the 2024 Pulitzer Prize for Music for his beautiful, understated Adagio (For Wadada Leo Smith.
Sorey also has, he says, ‘a lifelong connection to the ‘straight-ahead’ and the Great American Songbook which he shares with Diehl, with whom he has collaborated over many years, akin to ‘a brotherly connection’. Raghaven, highly respected and in-demand, has recently joined the trio.
A remarkable dialogue of exploration that flipped between pure abstract impressionism and an obliquely sidelong visit to the universe of mainstream jazz, setting invention and re-invention side by side." (Geoff Winston for UKJazzNews)

TYSHAWN SOREY TRIO live @ Stadtgarten, Köln | 2025
COLOGNE JAZZWEEK is a series by @berta.berlin and @colognejazzweek

Vanishing Twin is a London-based trio crafting surreal, boundary-blurring soundscapes at the intersection of experimental pop, minimalism, and dream-drenched psychedelia. Formed by singer and multi-instrumentalist Cathy Lucas in 2015, and now anchored by drummer Valentina Magaletti (Holy Tongue, Tomaga, Moin, Bat for Lashes) and bassist Susumu Mukai (Zongamin), the group has steadily honed a hypnotic and exploratory sound world over nearly a dozen releases.
"The group’s cosmopolitan membership initially reflected its mission to synthesize psychedelic traditions around the globe, from tropicalía to kosmische rock."

videos.title: DROGI KRAJOWE @ Jazzwerkstatt Peitz | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Drogi Krajowe is a Polish ensemble with members from Poznań and Toruń, which captivates audiences with its unusual instrumentation and sonic openness. The five musicians come from a wide variety of scenes – from contemporary classical to free improvisation – and combine jazz, punk, electronic, minimalism, and new music to create a unique sound. Since 2019, the band has been performing at festivals and clubs in Poland and Europe. In 2023, their debut album "Drogi Krajowe w Polsce" was released on the Multikulti Project label.

DROGI KRAJOWE @ Jazzwerkstatt Peitz | 2025
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: LYCHEE LASSI @ Kulturhaus Rofinpark | Jazz in E. 2025
videos.description:
The Berlin-based quartet fuse dub, bass, hip-hop, jazz, krautrock and psychedelic soundscapes into a hypnotic live experience that’s as raw as it is playful. Built around deep grooves, improvised textures and fearless experimentation, their music constantly mutates—tracks dissolve, reassemble and explode into new shapes. On stage LYCHEE LASSI thrives on tension and release: heavy basslines, driving beats, fractured samples and moments of sudden silence that pull the audience right to the edge. Nothing feels pre-packaged—everything is alive, bold and unpredictable.
Unconventional, immersive and impossible to pigeonhole, Lychee Lassi don’t just play concerts —they create sonic journeys. Berlin underground energy.

LYCHEE LASSI @ Kulturhaus Rofinpark, Eberswalde | 2025
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: VEHICLE PASSENGER live @ Stadtgarten, Köln | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2025
videos.description:
Ethereal yet full of raw power: Vehicle/Passenger combine jazz, hip-hop, and live electronics with spoken word in a haunting and incredibly modern sound. The international all-star trio, consisting of Caribbean M. Alberto (vocals, saxophone, and synthesizer), American drummer and percussionist Lesley Mok, and Cologne-based double bassist Florian Herzog, represents a tour de force of artistic expression. Alberto's thoughtful, radical poetry, which addresses, among other things, black experiences of alienation in a white society, makes its way through dynamic, powerful improvisations, collective grooves, and delicate soundscapes. Vehicle/Passenger's live performances convey an energy and urgency that is almost impossible to resist. According to All About Jazz, their latest album, Live in Amsterdam (2024), is “one of the most honest and unique recordings of the year.”

VEHICLE PASSENGER @ Stadtgarten, Köln | 2025
COLOGNE JAZZWEEK is a series by @berta.berlin and @colognejazzweek

Performer

videos.title: MARK ERNESTUS' NDAGGA RHYTHM FORCE @ GASWERK MUSIC DAYS | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Ndagga Rhythm Force are one of the most explosive African acts around. The project began in 2011, when Mark Ernestus (of Basic Channel, Rhythm & Sound, and Hard Wax fame) traveled to Senegal in search of original Mbalax recordings. A spontaneous session with over 20 percussionists in Dakar led to the 2012 twin albums 800% Ndagga and Ndagga Versions, released under the name “Mark Ernestus presents Jeri-Jeri.”
Since then, the group has evolved into a fierce live outfit, touring widely and earning acclaim for their raw, hypnotic energy. 
Their powerful new live show premiered at the Gaswerk Music Days with singer Mbene Diatta Seck, percussion legend Bada Seck, and key players from Dakar's vibrant music scene. 

https://www.gaswerk-music-days.de/

MARK ERNESTUS' NDAGGA RHYTM FORCE @ GASWERK MUSIC DAYS, Berlin  | 2025
A series by @berta.berlin and ‪@GaswerkMusicDays

videos.title: Ingebrigt Håker Flaten (Exit) Knarr @ Concert Hall of HfMT | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2025
videos.description:
The origins of (Exit) Knarr reveal a spark of creative synergy that has only grown stronger with time. For those familiar with Håker Flaten’s three-decade career as one of the finest musicians in jazz and improvised music, it was clear from the start that this project was something special. (Exit) Knarr is not just a band but a space for artistic collaboration, where great musicians come together to elevate one another and create something truly unique. This ethos is evident in the sheer musical joy captured on Drops. Few bands can match the vibrant energy and sense of discovery that (Exit) Knarr embodies, making it not just a thrill to hear but undoubtedly a joy to be part of. The band transforms abstract concepts into visceral, shape-shifting soundscapes rooted in jazz, improvisation, and electronic music.

(Exit) Knarr - live @ Concert Hall of HfMT, Köln | 2025
COLOGNE JAZZWEEK is a series by @berta.berlin and @colognejazzweek

videos.title: ZERALDA @ Hochschule für Musik "Hanns Eisler", Berlin | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
“Engelein, Engelein flieg!” (Engl.: “Little Angel, Fly!”) is a song from the upcoming album ZERALDA, which moves between chanson, influences from jazz and classical music, as well as grotesque–ecstatic beats. The album is composed for voice, violin, keys, drums, and bass by musician, performer, and director Wolke Milena Wilke. Inspired by Tomi Ungerer’s fairy tale "Zeralda's Ogre" the Album explores the conflict between a longing for sensual abundance and a deep connection to all living beings, and the fear o one’s own devouring and destructive potential. The album will be released in the course of 2026. 

ZERALDA @ Hochschule für Musik "Hanns Eisler", Berlin | 2025
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: ONOM AGEMO AND THE DISCO JUMPERS feat. AHMED AG KAEDY @ Jazzwerkstatt Peitz | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Ahmed Ag Kaedy, a musician from the nomadic Kel Tamasheq people and known from the film Mali Blues, has been working with the Berlin groove ensemble Onom Agemo and the Disco Jumpers since 2018. Under the direction of saxophonist Johannes Schleiermacher, a unique sound mix emerges from Tuareg traditions, explosive jazz improvisation, Krautrock, psychedelic soundscapes, and driving grooves. An intense dialogue between West Africa and Western Europe — powerful, creative, and absolutely unique.

videos.title: HAYDEN CHISHOLM - Checkpoint 50 Cologne @ Trinitatiskirche Cologne | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2025
videos.description:
Hayden Chisholm – Checkpoint 50 Cologne
Trinitatiskirche Cologne 01.09.2025
In advance I called this special concert "checkpoint 50" as a reference to the aid stations in ultra runs and the number I'd reached in life - I had no idea at the time it would turn out to be a very different kind of checkpoint, one I barely made, but such is the nature of long runs and life - at any turn storms can hit and they can hit hard. I was blessed to be given the chance to put together a dream team of musicians with the support of Janning Truman and the stellar team of the Cologne Jazz week. Every single musician on the stage is like a lighthouse in a screaming tempest, an impregnable fortress of smooth stone - the kind of musician when you are on stage with nothing can go astray and though I first planned to make two sets with the different groups, in the end we decided to just hit it as one charging light brigade with the heavy brass at the back and the quartet of Chemical Kinetics up front with Kit Downes on the organ on the left wing. As it was a big checkpoint I threw down everything dear to me - Fibonacci and Prime harmonics, New Zealand Taonga puoro, homages to teachers and mentors of old, and even some jazz music in between. I love the first 15 minutes when Petter simply sits like a monk and takes in the harmonics before he lifts his axe and kills the bass.
Thank you to Beat for his sweet single shot camera - a feat in itself that is a tribute to his musicality and intuition.
The last words I whisper are “Aroha ahau ki a koe" and "Kocham cię”- they both mean the same thing in Maori and Polish and my heart and voice barely had the strength to speak them out and pronounce them in full - I had already emptied myself. Yet however stuttered and broken they came out, they still did - and it could not have happened without the bastions of strength on my side. My heartfelt thanks you to all who got me through this checkpoint. A checkpoint is just that - a short respite and a chance for a deep breath and some electrolytes - hardly a moment for reflection let alone a tear. The next one is usually about 30kms away but it could be a lot longer. The end is nowhere near in sight. Till then we stay in the pain cave we know so well and keep grinding on. Breathe in breathe out. That’s what we do in my world, no matter what the number. And even if we have to do it alone, we do that.

HAYDEN CHISHOLM - Checkpoint 50 Cologne @ Trinitatiskirche, Cologne | 2025
COLOGNE JAZZWEEK is a series by ‪@berta.berlin  &‪ ‪@colognejazzweek

Pianist and composer Andrei Irimia creates profoundly introspective soundscapes that transcend traditional musical boundaries. His compositions are a delicate synergy between minimalist piano and the complexities of the human experience, inviting listeners to explore the depths within. His distinctive musical language evokes a spectrum of emotional states, from wistful melancholy and tender nostalgia to raw vulnerability and quiet revelation. Each composition unfolds as a journey, creating a sanctuary where time seems suspended and inner exploration becomes possible.

videos.title: KIT DOWNES @ KÖLNER DOM | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2025
videos.description:
Kit Downes is actually a jazz pianist. He formed his first piano trio in his first year at the renowned Rocal Academy Of Music in London. He quickly made a name for himself as a musician with a wide range of interests who curiously developed the vocabulary for his own jazz language. He became known for his trio ENEMY with Petter Eldh (bass) and James Maddren (drums), with whom he brought contemporary jazz to the stage, sometimes lyrically introspective, sometimes loudly eruptive. In 2018, he released ‘Obsidian’ on ECM, on which he can be heard exclusively on the church organ – a throwback to his own biography when he sat at this instrument as a teenager. As an improvisation artist of today, Downes now experiments with the different timbres of a church organ and often plays with them as if they were frequencies from analogue synthesizers. It will be interesting to see what the Briton does on the organ in Cologne Cathedral, which is one of the largest in Germany with its 143 stops.

KIT DOWNES @ KÖLNER DOM | 2025
COLOGNE JAZZWEEK is a series by ‪@berta.berlin‬ &‪ ‪@colognejazzweek‬

videos.title: BIŁAT – KOUATE @ JAZZEXZESS / House of Music | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Julia Biłat on cello & Dudù Kouate on drums, an extraordinary duo, presented by Jazzexzess. Berlin-based cellist, improviser, and composer Julia Biłat bridges classical music, jazz, performance, and free improvisation. Her cello becomes a resonating body for voice, movement, and space – at times delicate and fragile, at times powerful and eruptive.
Now a Berliner by choice, Dudù Kouate draws from the soundscapes of West African traditions. With drums, idiophones, and instruments made from recycled materials, he unfolds multifaceted rhythms and stories that weave together nature, culture, and his roots.
Not a concert in the classical sense – but a journey that sets both body and senses in motion.

BIŁAT – KOUATE @ JAZZEXZESS / House of Music, Berlin | 2025

videos.title: KAREN WILLEMS - Terre Sol Four - live @ Stadtgarten, Köln | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2025
videos.description:
Karen Willems originally started her ‘Terre Sol’ as a solo project with which the drummer and percussionist, born in Belgium in 1979, wanted to explore and reveal her real and imaginary roots. Her aim was to find music in which she could find herself as a spiritual being as well as a creative artist. She is concerned with the essence of her own work, the perception of her own personality and the further development of jazz as an art of fusing different aesthetics and genres. For the Belgian, jazz is also a communicative form of music that seeks dialogue and discourse in order to discover new things and explore the unknown. This is why Willems has added a ‘Four’ to her ‘Terre Sol’, so that from now on, as a quartet of four, she can set out in search of the essence of contemporary, improvised music and move sure-footedly along the borderline between jazz and improvised music, pop and rock, experimental music and musical avant-garde.

KAREN WILLEMS - Terre Sol Four - live @ Stadtgarten, Köln | 2025

videos.title: ANNIE BLOCH - I DEPEND  live @ artheater, Köln | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2025
videos.description:
The first instrument Annie Bloch played as a child was the church organ in her home town of Diepholz. Perhaps it was this instrument that later allowed her to write such hybrid and equally powerful and fragile music as a composer, because a church organ, with its various trades, also has a variety of sound colours that can be alienated and manipulated accordingly. As a singer-songwriter, Bloch, who now lives in Cologne, writes aesthetically and stylistically diverse pieces that are just as fond of the singability of folklore as they are of the artificial chords of jazz, the raw power of indie rock or the ritual rhythms of spiritual music. I DEPEND is an example of this. The songs are sometimes rhythmically fanned out, sometimes melodically leading, then again only perceptible as small pieces of a puzzle – and Bloch’s tentet is neither an orchestra nor a big band nor a large pop band.

ANNIE BLOCH - I DEPEND - live @ artheater, Köln | 2025
COLOGNE JAZZWEEK is a series by ‪@berta.berlin‬ &‪ ‪@colognejazzweek‬

videos.description:
Loom & Thread creates music based on samples. The sampler is played as a live instrument and merges with double bass, piano and drums in a unique real-time performance. The band's second album (out early 2026 on macro) is based on saxophone and vibraphone recordings that are atomized and recontextualized through a sampler. This creates a soundscape that initially resembles acoustic instruments but quickly undergoes significant transformations due to the sharp manipulations of the sampler.

videos.title: Maria Faust | Matteo Paggi  | Tilo Weber MORGENBARN @ LowSwing Studio, Berlin | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
Featuring the dynamic talents of drummer Tilo Weber from Germany, energetic saxophonist Maria Faust from Estonia, and extravagant trombonist Matteo Paggi from Italy, this super group is set to deliver an unforgettable performance of energetic free improvisation and original compositions.
With a shared passion for pushing musical boundaries, Weber, Faust, and Paggi bring their unique styles together to create a sound that is both captivating and innovative. Tilo Weber’s subtle yet powerful drumming lays a foundation that complements Faust’s vibrant, high-octane saxophone melodies. Matteo Paggi, known for his flamboyant trombone playing, adds a layer of richness and flair that takes the trio’s sound to new heights.
This collaboration was born from a mutual desire to explore the limitless possibilities of jazz, making their first performance at the Jazzfestival Alto Adige a must-see event. Audiences can expect a captivating blend of energetic improvisation, original works, and the spontaneous chemistry that arises from these three talented musicians.

MORGENBARN  @ LowSwing Studio, Berlin | 2025
FILMED BY EAR is a series by @berta.berlin

videos.title: Robert Landfermann - SOLO - live @ Basement / Christuskirche, Köln | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2025
videos.description:
It takes a lot for a double bass player to record his debut album as an unaccompanied solo improvisation. His debut ‘Null’ (‘Zero’) contains all the musical, aesthetic and technical qualities that have made Landfermann an internationally sought-after soloist ever since. You can sense his sense of adventure when he ventures into unknown territory. And yes, you also experience something of Landfermann’s first musical love: when the sixteenth-note chains clack furiously, his solo excursions also have something of heavy metal about them.

videos.title: THE HELIOCENTRICS @ Gaswerk Music Days | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
The Heliocentrics have long stirred a bewitching brew of funk, jazz, psych and library influences. They have played with a huge number of influential and diverse artists, from Marshall Allen and the Arkestra, Archie Shepp and The Gaslamp Killer, to Pharoahe Monch, Cut Chemist and Kareem Riggins.
Labeled by Pitchfork as “a live-band culmination of every obsessive hip-hop-era producer's drive to fall deeper into a rabbit hole of the previously unheard”, the band’s far-reaching influence meant a natural knock-on in being cited by hip-hop royalty: namely by DJ Shadow, and being sampled by Nas and Kanye Wes. The Heliocentrics released their last two LPs on Madlib's Invazion, Infinity of Now and Telemetric Sounds in 2020, a psychedelic collaboration with The Gaslamp Killer, ‘Legna’ in 2023. 

THE HELIOCENTRICS @ Gaswerk Music Days Festival, Berlin | 2024

videos.title: FULU MIZIKI @ Humboldtforum | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Percussion explosions, Afro-Futurism, punk attitude and environmental activism… the collective Fulu Miziki from Kinshasa throws all of this into the grinder and brings it to the stage at high speed. Fulu Miziki can be translated as ‘music made from waste’, which doesn’t just refer to the band’s instruments and masks, which they make themselves from various scrap materials. Fulu Miziki creates a rebellious sound from the everyday harshness of Kinshasa that cries out for concrete political change.

FULU MIZIKI - live @ Durchlüften Festival / Humboldtforum, Berlin , 2024
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: BIRGITTA FLICK QUARTET @ Studio Boerne, Berlin | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
The Birgitta Flick Quartet is regarded as one of the most exciting projects of the young German jazz generation thanks to its remarkable musical sensitivity and virtuosic joy of playing. Since the quartet set out on its improvisatory journey nearly 15 years ago, the band has released several critically acclaimed albums, including the latest release Miniatures & Fragments (2022) and Dalarna (2016) as a septet with Lina Nyberg, Silke Eberhard and Nico Lohmann, exploring a suite that Birgitta composed inspired by traditional pastoral music and chorales from Dalarna in Sweden. 
Over the years, they have developed and refined a unique improvisatory and explorative approach to whatever notational or other musical reference they interact with, be it Birgitta’s compositions or anything they like and are inspired by — thus inspiring also Birgitta’s current artistic research investigating the entangled creative activities within the practices of composing and improvising that this video is part of. It features four new compositions created through different processes of experimenting with the topics of co-creating and notating. 

BIRGITTA FLICK QUARTET @ Studio Boerne, Berlin | 2024
FILMED BY EAR is a series by @berta.berlin.

Performer
Andreas Schmidt - piano

videos.title: SOL-I SO @ JUNGE AKADEMIE, Akademie der Künste | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Composer and singer Sol-i So combines consciously as well as unconsciously various cultural influences, which have been shaped by her biography and encounters, to create her own musical language that defies classification. In four compositions, Sino-Korean traditions meet K-pop, Central European folk music meets soul, and English poems meet Asian musical philosophy. By placing unfamiliar and familiar sounds in relation to each other and bridging contrasts, So manages to make different voices audible.

Sol-i would like to thank the German, Korean, Hindu, Tamil, English, American, African-American, Italian, and Swiss people - all those whose culture, which they have lovingly shared, lives on.

SOL-I SO - live @ Akademie der Künste, Berlin | 2024
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: Fabiana Striffler & Karsten Hochapfel - La Plume Du Dimanche [album release show] | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Berlin violinist Fabiana Striffler and Parisian cellist Karsten Hochapfel share the same passion for improvisation, psychedelic folk, jazz, experimental music, but also for a simple song and the beauty of a melody. In “La Plume Du Dimanche” the two musicians move almost carelessly and intuitively outside of classical patterns, in search of a common form of expression, without entering into the maelstrom of the high culture of their respective instruments that has grown over centuries. This material created during this time documents the beginning of their collaboration and looks forward to further releases.

Fabiana Striffler & Karsten Hochapfel @ Intergalaktischer Kulturverein, Berlin | 2024
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: Becca Stevens & hear now berlin @ Heimathafen | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
This concert beautifully captures the outcome of Becca Stevens and Kelly Watson Woelffers longtime friendship. “Stevens has a beautifully nuanced vocal style, a delicate yet strongly assured delivery rooted in an open, broad, range which is very much her own, with the mildest of echoes of one of her heroines, Joni Mitchell, that also carries through to her guitar work ” HEAR NOW BERLIN - an outstanding contemporary ensemble from Berlin - create the chamber music of now, an immersive musical journey featuring special arrangements by Apollonio Maiello in this collaboration. “In the league of current female singer-songwriters, Stevens sits atop with a clear 15-point lead; her live performances (…) are simply among the best shows you will catch anywhere on the planet.” 

HEAR NOW BERLIN @ Heimathafen, Berlin | 2023
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: IGOR OSYPOV - "Hyper Oz" - live @ Gaswerksiedlung, Berlin | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
Highly acclaimed Guitarist, multi instrumentalist, composer and producer Igor Osypov with his crunchy and distortedly liquid sound has become one of the distinct and impressive voices in Jazz Indie-Rock, Hip-Hop and Fusion. His playing with “blood and guts” brings a “fresh and complete concept” (allaboutjazz.com) to concert stages and clubs all over the world.
As an Ukrainian exiled in Berlin, unable to return home, he is obliged to negotiate different cultural and political identities, and as a forward-looking contemporary musician he chooses to negotiate different genres and other less easily defined traditions. “Hyper Oz” is the newest project of Igor Osypov, where he is merging the sounds of underground beats, Electronica, Jazz and floaty rock guitar.

IGOR OSYPOV - "Hyper Oz" - live @ Gaswerksiedlung, Berlin | 2024
FILMED BY EAR is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: KRiSPER @ Zentrifuge | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
The project was initiated by Adi Stoenescu, an active presence in Romania in recent years. Following his relocation to Berlin, the group undergoes a fresh revitalization with an entirely new lineup, which includes highly talented musicians from the local scene.
KRiSPER combines layers of electric jazz, aggressive rock, and pop sensibility to generate a controlled explosion of sound. With an interest in improvisation, chaos, instability, and the structure of musical forms, the band is as attentive to the process that gives birth to their music as they are to the final product.

KRiSPER @ Zentrifuge, Berlin | 2024
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: MoE live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | A L'ARME! Festival 2023
videos.description:
A steamroller of sound, that is MoE. Frontwoman Guro Skumsnes Moe on the microphone and bass – a true legend of the punk/noise underground scene in Norway and beyond – has performed for the first time ever in Berlin with her longtime partner Håvard Skaset on the guitar. Special guests include drummer Veslemøy Narvesen and Mette Rasmussen on the saxophone. With this illustrious new line-up, the group will continue to work within the unwavering spirit of their own berserker creativity. According to MoE, you could call it “rock” if absolutely necessary, but really it’s so much more than that. The deck has been reshuffled, anything is possible.

MoE live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | A L'ARME! Festival 2023
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: GRÉGOIRE SIMON @ DARA String Festival - Theater im Delphi | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
DARA String Festival presents a wide range of innovative, genre-bending music for string instruments, ranging from early contemporary music to experimental
and freely improvised music. DARA transcends genre boundaries, bringing together high-caliber international artists from diverse musical backgrounds in a series of encounters to stimulate new thoughts and ideas for the advancement of individual and collective artistic endeavors.

Funded by Musikfonds e.V. by means of the Federal Government Commissioner for Culture and the Media, and INM Berlin. 
Gefördert vom Musikfonds e.V. mit Projektmitteln der Beauftragten der Bundesregierung für Kultur und Medien, und INM Berlin. 

GRÉGOIRE SIMON @ DARA String Festival - Theater im Delphi, Berlin | 2023
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

Performer
Grégoire Simon - Viola, Electronics, Percussion

Grégoire Simon works between Berlin and Paris. After studying violin and viola he was chosen by Pierre Boulez to take the solo viola position in the prestigious Ensemble Intercontemporain (Paris). Half of the electronic duo Yes Soeur! Grégoire Simon has been producing original music for visual installations and contemporary dance shows. With his Berlin based project SUGAR, Grégoire Simon built bridges between chamber music and pop music with original creations. 
Simon obtained various composition commissions played by the Ensemble Flashback (Perpignan), Solistenensemble Kaleidoskop, Zafraan Ensemble and Andromeda Mega Express Orchestra.

videos.description:
Nick Dunston is a Berlin based composer and performer. His work touches on ideas such as embodiment, ancestral memory, and Afrosurrealism. On Skultura, he continuously shapes the music with various sound-manipulation techniques to create an album that is meticulous as it is visceral. In addition to his broad solo practice, he also constantly engages with improvisers and artists of other mediums as a means of cultivating an inquisitive, experimental, and beautiful body of collaborative works.

videos.description:
SANTROFI is a dynamic collective of celebrated young musicians from Accra’s music scene, gathered together by bassist and producer, Emmanuel Ofori, known for his work with Highlife legend, Ebo Taylor and Pat Thomas’s Kwashibu Area Band. And THE OMNIVERSAL EARKESTRA is an unusual big band as well. There is no bandleader, the musical direction changes constantly. videos.description:
HEAR NOW BERLIN is a virtuosic, post-genre chamber sextet, creating original music and playing works by some of today's most cutting-edge composers. Merging contemporary classical, avant-garde, jazz and pop, they create the chamber music of now. In their concert they have compiled a series of compositions that illuminate various aspects of human life, creating an immersive musical journey. Excerpts from a concert with music by US composers Judd Greenstein and Nathan Schramm and a new piece by flutist Kelly Watson Woelffer.

videos.title: WITCH 'N' MONK @ FLUID FORM CLUB #2 | ausland, Berlin | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
With a touch of Frank Zappa lyricism and a radical punk aesthetic, the duo of Heidi Heidelberg and Mauricio Velasierra crafts a dramatically bizarre vocal music from another dimension! Dreamlike improvisations are sampled, manipulated and reinvented through the lens of contemporary music and pop. You have never heard anything quite like Witch ’n’ Monk!" (John Zorn) 
Radically electrified Latin American flutes with jazz-punk guitar/bass and anarchic soprano vocals. Creating contrast is fundamental to their work and thus they tread upon the edges of genres. Their latest album was named Contemporary Album of the Month by The Guardian and received the prestigious Preis der deutschen Schallplattenkritik.

Fluid Form Club presents passionately unpredictable music that escapes classification, for a community of curious minds.
Funded by Musikfonds e.V. by means of the Federal Government Commissioner for Culture and the Media

LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: Christoph Möckel Trio - live @ Bonello Studio, Berlin | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
An exciting electric trio in the rare line-up of bass-clarinet/saxophone, electric bass and drums. The extensive use of electronic live effects allows for an almost limitless sound exploration and leads to a homogeneous and surprising fusion that ranges from ambient to free to experimental jazz(rock). According to Deutschlandfunk, Christoph Möckel, one of the "most promising young German saxophonists". Together with Oliver Lutz and Moritz Baumgärtner they represent a new generation of young innovative jazz musicians from Berlin and Cologne. They allow imaginative soundscapes to flourish in highly energetic improvisations and interactions. Creative jazz on the pulse of time!

videos.title: ERNTE - live @ Chez Chérie Studio, Berlin | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
The harvest is in! Five jazz musicians dedicate themselves to a repertoire of the 20th centur, harvest of their (crisis) time: resistance songs, workers' songs, partisan songs and anti-fascist fight songs. That is why, true to the motto "he who sows the wind will reap the storm", ERNTE plays music with and in protest. Without lyrics, but with great joy of playing and everything that jazz has to offer for this: Improvisation, unpredictability, energy, transcendence and a trace of melancholy. 

ERNTE - live @ Chez Chérie Studio, Berlin | 2022
FILMED BY EAR is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: RAMEEN SAQIZADA @ Industriesalon Schöneweide | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Rubab, also known as Rabab is one of the national musical instruments of Afghanistan. It is also known as ‘the lion of instruments’. This instrument is made with a single piece of wood from the mulberry tree. This melodious instrument is immensely famous in the area of Afghanistan, Pakistan, Kashmir, and a few regions in north India. It is vastly used in the traditional musical forms of the regions. It is a proven entertaining instrument in all these places.
Ustad Rameen Saqizada is one of the profound masters of Rubab.

Rameen Saqizada - live @ Industriesalon Schöneweide, Berlin | 2023
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.
This piece has been performed within "Reinventing Kabul" - watch more here: https://youtu.be/ZVxosBs9cXE

Performer

Fridoon Miazada – Tabla

videos.title: TRAINING with RUTH GOLLER @ Factory Studio, Berlin | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
TRAINING played at Moers Festival, at Jazzfest Berlin (in transatlantic cooperation with John Dieterich - from Deerhoof), at Eurojazz Mexico, Saalfelden Jazz Festival, as well as numerous club tours. Furthermore they created a concert series with a wide variety of guests in Berlin called Group TRAINING. Ruth Goller is a London-based bassist who has been involved in the development of many British experimental projects. Her musical focus has been in recent years on the development of her own project "SKYLLA", in which many of her influences get together. 
Ruth's style of composing includes microtonal bass harmonics with layers of vocal harmonies.
The three create a new, energetic music collectively: Meandering grooves, complex instrumental-vocal nestings, expressive training units, weightless unison melodies.

TRAINING with Ruth Goller - live @ Factory Studio, Berlin | 2023
FILMED BY EAR is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.description:
The whole breathes passion, desire, delirium, healthy madness and overflowing imaginations. Sometimes in the vicinity of Blonde Redhead, the Velvet, Deerhoof, regularly drawn to the slopes of Sonic Youth or The Ex, Pili Coït doesn't reduce itself to these references and generates its own aesthetic. videos.title: DLW | Dell-Lillinger-Westergaard @ Stueler Kirche, Peitz
videos.description:
Exploring new musical aesthetics, the internationally acclaimed composer trio Dell-Lillinger-Westergaard walks the line between contemporary, experimental and Avantgarde music.
Their music stands out due to its energetic intensity, physicality and architectural complexity. Breaking all genre boundaries, it has great depth of zoom and plasticity. Through a highly immersive process the music is simultaneously examined and created, highlighting and creating a fascinating world of vivid tonal and structural wealth.

videos.description:

videos.title: HYPNAGOGIA @ Atelier Wolfgang Flad | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
HYPNAGOGIA - the transition between wakefulness and sleep, control and release. 
Mbira and Maqam tonality meet African Jazz. An encounter between classical European instruments, bass clarinet, trumpet, flugelhorn and mbira, a traditional instrument from Southern Africa. Together they weave a unique new musical texture, beautifully relaxed and loaded with on point musicality. Lively warmth and shiny metallic buzz.

videos.title: Nils Wogram "Muse" - live @ Sendesaal, Bremen | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
Nils Wogram's music has always had a great deal of sensuality, and in this project that means all the more: All or nothing! Sound is the key component. Trombone, saxophone, harp, viola and voice are the basis for this. The visionary, Nils Wogram, who never just searches for the sake of searching, but who makes the impossible possible and the unheard of, without any compromise.
The essence is the great sensitivity with which the musicians listen to each other, how they react and interact to the sounds they create together. 
This music is lyrical and full of poetry. Some kind kind of improvised chamber music with an affinity to film, contemporary classical, folk and jazz.
Deutscher Jazzpreis/German Jazz-Award 2022 - Album Instrumental

videos.description:
This extraordinary percussion ensemble around Francesco D'Auria and Günter "Baby" Sommer have been around for 35 years: The magical world of percussion, the ongoing search for new sounds from the instruments, the somnambulistic connection between the musicians - all this makes Percussion Staff so unique. The song of the elements of drum skins, wood, metal, water is joined by the melodic sound of marimba and vibraphone, trumpet and flugelhorn, whistle and shell and human voice, combine to form music that smells of the sea and olives, in which one can Hear the buzzing of the crickets.

NAKED RECORDS launch concert presented by the Deutsches Theater. 
It's best to listen to this music for the first time in its entirety, from beginning to end. Feel its message, sense the fragments that are (probably for everyone, different) the most fascinating. Actually, everything here is fascinating. The rhythmic repetitions, the pauses in between, carried by a single sound, or everything at once. A rhythm that keeps you breathless and doesn't let go. An absolute must-listen (album)! - jazz-fun.de

The project was initiated by Adi Stoenescu, an active presence in Romania in recent years. Following his relocation to Berlin, the group undergoes a fresh revitalization with an entirely new lineup, which includes highly talented musicians from the local scene.
KRiSPER combines layers of electric jazz, aggressive rock, and pop sensibility to generate a controlled explosion of sound. With an interest in improvisation, chaos, instability, and the structure of musical forms, the band is as attentive to the process that gives birth to their music as they are to the final product.

videos.title: JOHANSSON & JELINEK  - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | A L'ARME! FESTIVAL 2024
videos.description:
Sven-Åke Johansson is one of the heroes of the European free jazz scene of the 1960s. Now in his 80s, he remains active in a wide variety of contexts and collaborations. Johansson has been working with Jan Jelinek, a kindred spirit in the field of electronic music, since 2017. The pair’s core concept is to confront Johansson’s drum playing, made up of simple, flowing, organically proliferating figures, with Jelinek’s modular synthesizer: the assumed regularity of machines with the small deficits of human action. The duo combines two generations and two tonal languages that are linked by a shared sensibility. Johansson is imbued with the anarchist spirit of jazz and trusts in obscure, even absurd ideas, while Jelinek understands how to constantly recontextualize the micro-textures of rhythm and sound.

JOHA'NSSON & JELINEK @ A L'ARME! Festival 2024 | Radialsystem, Berlin | 2024
A series by @berta.berlin and A L'ARME! Festival.
Watch all concert films here: https://www.alarmefestival.de/concert-films-alarme

videos.title: Ernst Reijseger & Mario Forte - live @ St. Agnes Church | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2024
videos.description:
The French-Italian violinist Mario Forte, who was born in Algeria and now lives in New York, and the Dutch cellist Ernst Reijseger form an exceptionally exciting duo. Forte studied with the French jazz violinist Didier Lockwood, teaches as a professor at the University of Lausanne and co-founded the Artempo Festival as artistic director, composer and performer. Reijseger has released more than 50 albums to date, including with major Dutch and international jazz avant-gardists such as Misha Mengelberg, Han Bennink and Steve Lacy, as well as with classical performers such as Yo Yo Ma, Giovanni Sollima and the Dutch Wind Ensemble. He has written film music for Werner Herzog and Shakespeare’s “Hamlet”, directed by Sam Gold. On their joint album “Fata Morgana”, they experiment with acoustic-traditional sounds and electronic alienation, jazz, baroque music, minimal music, improvisation and conception.

Ernst Reijseger & Mario Forte - live @ live @ St. Agnes, Köln | 2024
COLOGNE JAZZWEEK is a series by @berta.berlin & ‪@colognejazzweek‬

videos.title: FELIX HENKELHAUSEN "Deranged Particles" @ JAZZEXZESS / House of Music | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Felix Henkelhausen presents his latest project Deranged Particles with some of his closest collaborators, including tenor saxophonist Philipp Gropper, trumpeter Percy Pursglove and vibraphonist Evi Filippou. The project’s title, Deranged Particles is emblematic of the band’s musical concept, describing the state of abstract particles that are in motion independently but collide from time to time; blurring the lines between the individual and the collective. With this particular constellation, Deranged Particles makes way for both percussive complexity and colourful expressiveness.

FELIX HENKELHAUSEN "Deranged Particles" @ JAZZEXZESS / House of Music, Berlin | 2024
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: KUU! - live @ Stadtgarten Köln | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2024
videos.description:
A few years ago, singer and actress Jelena Kuljić, born in 1976 in Zrenanjin, Serbia, performed in theatre productions with Berlin-based Finnish guitarist Kalle Kalima. During the breaks, the two experimented with all kinds of new music, rock and jazz – and realised that the proverbial chemistry between them was right. This duo, which was formed by chance, was soon to be given a broader framework as KUU! Kuljić and Kalima brought in aesthetically adequate partners in guitarist Frank Möbus and drummer Christian Lillinger, who are indeed capable of spanning a wide stylistic range with them on an ad hoc basis. KUU! is now a collectively organised and stylistically eclectic band that unabashedly takes up controversial political themes in its lyrics and processes them in a sometimes loudly rocking, sometimes harshly grooving and often tonally varied ambience.

KUU! - live @ Stadtgarten, Köln | 2024
COLOGNE JAZZWEEK is a series by @berta.berlin & ‪@colognejazzweek‬

videos.title: DIEGO PINERA "Odd Wisdom" @ Zentrifuge, Berlin | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
Diego Piñera has been nominated several times both internationally and nationally as a drummer ‘instrumentalist’ and as a songwriter since 2005. Born in Montevideo, he took part in jam sessions at the age of three and later studied at the University of Music in Havana and at Berklee College. His album ‘My Picture’ was described as a ‘masterpiece by one of the most interesting drummers of our time’. Diego Piñera establishes his style here, a combination of ‘odd metres’, crooked beats and melodic drumset entries. The bandleader and composer just recently released his new album ‘Underground Roller Coaster'!

DIEGO PINERA @ Zentrifuge, Berlin | 2024

videos.title: ØKSE @ Jazzwerkstatt Peitz | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
The quartet ØKSE, which was founded by Harris and Rasmussen in 2022 for the Jazzfestival Saalfelden, combines complex parameter values of drums, bass, electronics, and saxophone and adds elements of free jazz, punk, Krautrock, and guttural vocal hymns.
The band’s name promises that none of this will sound overly mathematical for a single second, even against this theoretical background: Økse is the Danish word for “axe,” one of humanity’s oldest tools and one that, in case of doubt, can be used to break through any and every sonic knot.

ØKSE - live @ Jazzwerkstatt Festival, Peitz | 2024
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: JOCHEN RUECKERT QUINTET - live @ Stadtgarten Köln | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2024
videos.description:
Word spread quickly in New York about Jochen Rueckert in the city in 1998 – pianist Marc Copland brought the German into his trio, guitarist Kurt Rosenwinkel hired him, as did saxophonist Mark Turner. Rueckert kept in touch with Europe through a quartet with former fellow students from Cologne: Root 70 around trombonist Nils Wogram. He put together a quintet for his concert at the Cologne Jazzweek, with which he makes some of his songs sound like they were played by a big band. Seamus Blake is there on tenor, and together with bassist Doug Weiss he represents the American side of this quintet. Europe is responsible for the low wind frequencies: with Wogram, of course, and the Dutchman Joris Roelofs on bass clarinet. And Rueckert is the primus inter pares on drums – what more could you want?

JOCHEN RUECKERT QUINTET - live @ Stadtgarten Köln | 2024
COLOGNE JAZZWEEK is a series by @berta.berlin & ‪@colognejazzweek‬

videos.title: OS BARBAPAPAS @ JAZZEXZESS / House of Music | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Formed by four friends – one woman and three men – the Brazilian band blends Afro-Brazilian rhythms with a unique contemporary sound, combining the vibrant energy of drums with Latin elements and world music. The highlight is the inclusion of glass harps, a distinctive instrument that produces ethereal sounds and transforms each performance into an almost ritualistic experience. This fusion of tradition and innovation makes the band a standout in Brazilian music, seamlessly connecting the past and the present in a mesmerizing way.

videos.title: THE KILLING POPES - live @ JAKI, Köln | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2024
videos.description:
Berlin-based drummer Oliver Steidle is adding a political and socially critical edge to their improvised music when they mix contemporary jazz with hip-hop, grindcore or punk for example. Steidle describes the music of his quartet The Killing Popes as “future jazz”. The original pieces follow a “cut & paste” aesthetic that often creates sharp contrasts in the improvised music. Anticipating each other, the musicians constantly take up new positions at breakneck speed, like pieces in speed chess, making it a joy for everyone to follow the action on stage with their eyes and ears. But despite all the harmonic, rhythmic and melodic complexity, the four musicians show above all how humorous and witty today’s avant-garde jazz music can sound.

THE KILLING POPES - live @ Jaki, Köln | 2024
COLOGNE JAZZWEEK is a series by @berta.berlin & ‪@colognejazzweek‬

videos.title: PLEXUS - Armbruster & Heck live @ Filmhaus Köln | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2024
videos.description:
PLEXUS with trombonist Carlotta Armbruster and drummer Jonas Heck is an innovative element in the young contemporary jazz scene. In their duo, the two Cologne-based musicians explore the possibilities of their unconventional instrumentation without being limited by harmony and bass instruments. This allows them to make fresh, improvisational and interactive music based on contemporary pieces and original compositions. Unfamiliar sounds, new structures and an intensive dialog lead the listeners on paths away from established listening habits. Form and structure are created through a balanced relationship between abstractness, clear motifs and catchy melodies, and the duo has already won several awards. It won the main prize in the “Jugend Jazzt” competition in 2022 and was a prizewinner at the renowned international “Biberacher Jazzpreis”. In 2023, PLEXUS recorded its debut album for Deutschlandfunk.

PLEXUS - live @ Filmhaus Köln | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2024
COLOGNE JAZZWEEK is a series by @berta.berlin & ‪@colognejazzweek‬

videos.title: CINNA PEYGHAMY @ Gaswerk Music Days | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Cinna Peyghamy is a composer and sound artist, born in France with Iranian parents. At the Gaswerk Music Days he took us on a mesmerizing journey with his live act that marries the traditional resonance of tombak with the avant-garde possibilities of a modular synthesizer. His improvised sequences unfold seamlessly, intertwining Persian percussion and electronic innovation. Peyghamy’s mastery of the tombak provides a rhythmic foundation that resonates with cultural depth, while the modular synthesizer adds an ethereal, futuristic layer, creating a harmonious blend that defies genre boundaries.

The legendary band LYCHEE LASSI from Berlin is celebrating its 25th anniversary with a tour in their original formation. Based, Beat, Berger, and DJ illvibe are reuniting to present their unique mix of hip hop beats, turntablism, and jam band improvisation live once again. LYCHEE LASSI is blending hip hop with jazz, kraut and funk. videos.title: KUHN FU - live @ Stadtgarten Köln | COLOGNE JAZZWEEK 2024
videos.description:
KUHN FU is the name Berlin guitarist Christian Achim Kühn has given his multinational band, with which he has been causing a furore in the truest sense of the word on the stages of clubs and festivals since it was founded in 2012. The whole performance seems refreshingly crude and absurd, it has a lot of the anarchy of Dadaism and Surrealism, while the music is raw and mangy, shaggy and unbrushed. But that’s also what jazz has to be today: cheeky, bold, nosy.

videos.title: THE HELIOCENTRICS @ Gaswerk Music Days | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
The Heliocentrics have long stirred a bewitching brew of funk, jazz, psych and library influences. They have played with a huge number of influential and diverse artists, from Marshall Allen and the Arkestra, Archie Shepp and The Gaslamp Killer, to Pharoahe Monch, Cut Chemist and Kareem Riggins.
Labeled by Pitchfork as “a live-band culmination of every obsessive hip-hop-era producer's drive to fall deeper into a rabbit hole of the previously unheard”, the band’s far-reaching influence meant a natural knock-on in being cited by hip-hop royalty: namely by DJ Shadow, and being sampled by Nas and Kanye Wes. The Heliocentrics released their last two LPs on Madlib's Invazion, Infinity of Now and Telemetric Sounds in 2020, a psychedelic collaboration with The Gaslamp Killer, ‘Legna’ in 2023. 

THE HELIOCENTRICS @ Gaswerk Music Days Festival, Berlin | 2024
A series by @berta.berlin and @GaswerkMusicDays

videos.title: FULU MIZIKI @ Humboldtforum | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Percussion explosions, Afro-Futurism, punk attitude and environmental activism… the collective Fulu Miziki from Kinshasa throws all of this into the grinder and brings it to the stage at high speed. Fulu Miziki can be translated as ‘music made from waste’, which doesn’t just refer to the band’s instruments and masks, which they make themselves from various scrap materials. Fulu Miziki creates a rebellious sound from the everyday harshness of Kinshasa that cries out for concrete political change.

videos.title: STRIFFLER & HOCHAPFEL & FRIENDS @ Intergalaktischer Kulturverein | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Berlin violinist Fabiana Striffler and Parisian cellist Karsten Hochapfel share the same passion for improvisation, psychedelic folk, jazz, experimental music, but also for a simple song and the beauty of a melody. In “La Plume Du Dimanche” the two musicians move almost carelessly and intuitively outside of classical patterns, in search of a common form of expression, without entering into the maelstrom of the high culture of their respective instruments that has grown over centuries. This material created during this time documents the beginning of their collaboration and looks forward to further releases.

STRIFFLER & HOCHAPFEL & FRIENDS - live @ Intergalaktischer Kulturverein, Berlin | 2024
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: BIRGITTA FLICK QUARTET @ Studio Boerne, Berlin | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
The Birgitta Flick Quartet is regarded as one of the most exciting projects of the young German jazz generation thanks to its remarkable musical sensitivity and virtuosic joy of playing. Since the quartet set out on its improvisatory journey nearly 15 years ago, the band has released several critically acclaimed albums, including the latest release Miniatures & Fragments (2022) and Dalarna (2016) as a septet with Lina Nyberg, Silke Eberhard and Nico Lohmann, exploring a suite that Birgitta composed inspired by traditional pastoral music and chorales from Dalarna in Sweden. 
Over the years, they have developed and refined a unique improvisatory and explorative approach to whatever notational or other musical reference they interact with, be it Birgitta’s compositions or anything they like and are inspired by — thus inspiring also Birgitta’s current artistic research investigating the entangled creative activities within the practices of composing and improvising that this video is part of. It features four new compositions created through different processes of experimenting with the topics of co-creating and notating. 

BIRGITTA FLICK QUARTET @ Studio Boerne, Berlin | 2024
FILMED BY EAR is a series by @berta.berlin.

Performer

James Banner - bass

videos.title: SOL-I SO @ JUNGE AKADEMIE, Akademie der Künste | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Composer and singer Sol-i So combines consciously as well as unconsciously various cultural influences, which have been shaped by her biography and encounters, to create her own musical language that defies classification. In four compositions, Sino-Korean traditions meet K-pop, Central European folk music meets soul, and English poems meet Asian musical philosophy. By placing unfamiliar and familiar sounds in relation to each other and bridging contrasts, So manages to make different voices audible.

Sol-i would like to thank the German, Korean, Hindu, Tamil, English, American, African-American, Italian, and Swiss people - all those whose culture, which they have lovingly shared, lives on.

SOL-I SO - live @ Akademie der Künste, Berlin | 2024
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: WNBL @ Club Gretchen - 2024 | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
wnbl, formerly known as Wanubalé, is a collective of nine young musicians based in Berlin and Potsdam, who bring an unique blend of influences from electronic music, jazz and hiphop with a matchless energy onto the stage. Their constantly evolving approach to music results in a blissful room capturing harmony of virtuously played music that makes everybody feel like dancing in the blink of an eye. With a brass section of four and a rhythm section that has two drummers their live sets guarantee for infectious playfulness, exciting improvisations and ecstatic beats.

videos.title: RICHARD KOCH'S EMPOWERMENT TRIO @ Kloster Lehnin | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
With its catchy melodies, drum machine beats and daring improvisations, the Empowerment Trio creates a danceable and at the same time sophisticated concert experience.
The producer and bassist Joh Weisgerber (Flo Mega and the Ruffcats), pianist and Jazzlab founder Nicolas Schulze as well as the trumpeter and bandleader Richard Koch (Beatsteaks, Nils Frahm, Jimi Tenor) open your ears with their impressive interaction and invite you to celebrate! 

Filmed at Kunstort Lehnin, Kloster Lehnin, Brandenburg-Germany with kind support from Ministerium für Wissenschaft, Forschung und Kultur Brandenburg.
Special thanks to Joh Weisgerber, berta.berlin, David Thibaut, Thorsten Müller, Anja Thurm, Julia Winter, Martin Grade, Marius Haberland and Elias Haisch. All compositions by Richard Koch.

videos.title: Fabiana Striffler & Karsten Hochapfel - La Plume Du Dimanche [album release show] | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Berlin violinist Fabiana Striffler and Parisian cellist Karsten Hochapfel share the same passion for improvisation, psychedelic folk, jazz, experimental music, but also for a simple song and the beauty of a melody. In “La Plume Du Dimanche” the two musicians move almost carelessly and intuitively outside of classical patterns, in search of a common form of expression, without entering into the maelstrom of the high culture of their respective instruments that has grown over centuries. This material created during this time documents the beginning of their collaboration and looks forward to further releases.

Fabiana Striffler & Karsten Hochapfel @ Intergalaktischer Kulturverein, Berlin | 2024
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: TRYON - live @ Kühlspot Social Club, Berlin | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
TRYON is a large ensemble project based on the written compositions of Kellen Mills. It combines complex written form as well as free improvisation and classical avant-garde. “Jazz rock and rock sounds and rhythms can at one point morph into jazz avant-garde or emotional and even tender statements. At other moments, the musicians play intellectually and sonically demanding or meet at a certain point on the level of the classic jazz form. There are no rules here. There is a wonderful, seemingly spontaneous game.“ (Jazzfun.de)

videos.title: HERTENSTEIN TRIO with ACHIM KAUFMANN @ Sowieso | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
The debut album of this international modern jazz trio around Berlin-NYC-based drummer, composer Joe Hertenstein featuring Amsterdam-based reeds man, composer Michael Moore “LIVE DURING LOCKDOWN” was voted “Best Live Album 2022” by The NYC Jazz Record. Newly completed by Berlin-based, Italian bassist Antonio Borghini, this trio’s music spans from swing to abstraction presenting the most beautiful of original melodies, deepest grooves and interactions, and most exciting flights of improvisations. This evening at Berlin’s club Sowieso, the “Joe Hertenstein Trio feat. Michael Moore” invited the great pianist Achim Kaufmann for the adventure of a quartet expansion to a packed house.

videos.title: KASSA OVERALL  - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | A L'ARME! FESTIVAL 2023
videos.description:
One of the brightest stars of the young New York jazz scene brought A L’ARME! Vol X+I to its concertante conclusion: Kassa Overall is a drummer, singer, MC, and producer, as fluent in the vocabulary of hip hop as with jazz traditions, well-acquainted with the underground and avant garde scenes in Brooklyn. His latest album Animals has been celebrated as the greatest masterpiece of “one of modern jazz music’s most audacious futurists” (Pitchfork). NPR described it as “the sound of whole new lanes opening up.” Overall sees all of his projects as a challenge of curating and arranging human chemistry. Having worked together with Angela Davis, Vijay Iyer, and Yoko Ono, among others, this time Tomoki Sanders joined the well-oiled machine that is Kassas band. The non-binary musician inherited their saxophone from none other than their father, Pharoah Sanders.

KASSA OVERALL @ A L'ARME! Festival 2023, Radialsystem, Berlin | 2023
A series by @berta.berlin and A L'ARME! Festival.
Watch all concert films here: https://www.alarmefestival.de/concert-films-alarme

videos.title: NIESCIER – REID – HARRIS @ JAZZEXZESS / House of Music | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Always seeking new musical adventures, Niescier is one of the most distinguished voices on the European jazz scene. Together with renowned cellist Tomeka Reid and acclaimed drummer Savannah Harris, three musicians with sparkling temperament, drive and a joy for experimentation are united to let the complexity and originality of Niescier‘s compositions shine. This trio exists in a state of perpetual motion, always a bit ephemeral, never revealing all there is and even when they reduce the tone to an urgent whisper, the music makes its presence felt.
(Dave Sumner, Daily Bandcamp)

NIESCIER – REID – HARRIS @ JAZZEXZESS / House of Music, Berlin | 2024

videos.title: SELVHENTER @ JAZZEXZESS / House of Music | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Going past musical genres and instead straight towards something more elemental – Selvhenter’s
music creates a strikingly direct, physical experience of sound composed of polyrhythms,
acoustic and electric melodies, heavy music, and improvised beauty.
Since forming in Copenhagen in 2010, drummers Jaleh Negari and Anja Jacobsen, saxophonist
Sonja LaBianca, and trombonist Maria Bertel have forged a unique approach to making music
that starts with their instrumental setup – 2 x drummers that interlock as frequently as they go
their own way, a trombone put through a bass amplifier loud enough to rattle your chest, and a
saxophone put through a range of effects so that it often sounds unrecognizable, but also at times
where you think: “This is what a saxophone is made for”.

SELVHENTER @ Jazzexzess / House of Music, Berlin | 2024
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

Performer
Sonja LaBianca - Saxophone
Maria Bertel - Trombone
Anja Jacobsen - Drums

videos.description:
RE:CALAMARI - Modern Creative Jazz Rock from Amsterdam, Cologne and Berlin: a “collective definition of the location of newer jazz” that obviously refers to the best of 70ies Fusion. It embodies a pulsating unit of four exceptional musicians, led by Cologne bassist Oliver Lutz, promising “a little more of everything” and they have been described as the "apotheosis of historic European jazz liberation". videos.title: Becca Stevens & hear now berlin @ Heimathafen | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
This concert beautifully captures the outcome of Becca Stevens and Kelly Watson Woelffers longtime friendship. “Stevens has a beautifully nuanced vocal style, a delicate yet strongly assured delivery rooted in an open, broad, range which is very much her own, with the mildest of echoes of one of her heroines, Joni Mitchell, that also carries through to her guitar work ” HEAR NOW BERLIN - an outstanding contemporary ensemble from Berlin - create the chamber music of now, an immersive musical journey featuring special arrangements by Apollonio Maiello in this collaboration. “In the league of current female singer-songwriters, Stevens sits atop with a clear 15-point lead; her live performances (…) are simply among the best shows you will catch anywhere on the planet.”

videos.title: LUCÍA MARTÍNEZ & THE FEARLESS @ Donau 115 | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
An explorer of the freest jazz and a prodigal composer of film music, drummer Lucía Martínez is essentially a cosmopolitan artist who does not impose her own horizons.
Her Berlin project with The Fearless is a good summary of all this: from Sergio Leone and Morricone inspired melodies and from there to Bruce Lee, supported by an instrumentation chosen with excellent criteria - bass clarinet, horn, guitar even electronics set - to embrace a wide variety of textures and expressive possibilities, and to offer a suggestive and moving canvas.
Modern jazz, with elements of improvised music, 'Lucía Martínez & The Fearless' shows an artist at one of her best creative moments.

LUCÍA MARTÍNEZ & THE FEARLESS - live @ Donau 115, Berlin | 2024
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: IGOR OSYPOV - "Hyper Oz" - live @ Gaswerksiedlung, Berlin | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
Highly acclaimed Guitarist, multi instrumentalist, composer and producer Igor Osypov with his crunchy and distortedly liquid sound has become one of the distinct and impressive voices in Jazz Indie-Rock, Hip-Hop and Fusion. His playing with “blood and guts” brings a “fresh and complete concept” (allaboutjazz.com) to concert stages and clubs all over the world.
As an Ukrainian exiled in Berlin, unable to return home, he is obliged to negotiate different cultural and political identities, and as a forward-looking contemporary musician he chooses to negotiate different genres and other less easily defined traditions. “Hyper Oz” is the newest project of Igor Osypov, where he is merging the sounds of underground beats, Electronica, Jazz and floaty rock guitar.

IGOR OSYPOV - "Hyper Oz" - live @ Gaswerksiedlung, Berlin | 2024

videos.title: KRiSPER @ Zentrifuge | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
The project was initiated by Adi Stoenescu, an active presence in Romania in recent years. Following his relocation to Berlin, the group undergoes a fresh revitalization with an entirely new lineup, which includes highly talented musicians from the local scene.
KRiSPER combines layers of electric jazz, aggressive rock, and pop sensibility to generate a controlled explosion of sound. With an interest in improvisation, chaos, instability, and the structure of musical forms, the band is as attentive to the process that gives birth to their music as they are to the final product.

videos.title: 1 ABOVE MINUS UNDERGROUND live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | A L'ARME! Festival 2023
videos.description:
A soundtrack for those invested in unrest – that is how Lukas Koenig describes 1 Above Minus Underground, the musical manifesto that he recorded together with vocalists and musicians from the US, the UK, Iran, and Austria and released in April 2023. At A L’ARME!, he brought these troublemaking sounds to the stage with a full band for the first time ever: a foundation of massive hip-hop beats, synthesizers, and experimental noise, over which Elvin Brandhi, Dälek, and Rojin Sharafi pile up energetic raps and evocative rhymes. Their lyrics revolve around grievances and political imbalances, while Koenig on the drums, Nik Hummer on the modular synthesizer, and Victoria Shen with her one-of-a-kind nail-needle scratch techniques continue to stoke the revolution. Nothing less than a declaration of war.

1 ABOVE MINUS UNDERGROUND live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | A L'ARME! Festival 2023
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

Performer
LUKAS KOENIG (AT) — DRUMS, SYNTHESIZER
DÄLEK (US) — VOCALS
VICTORIA SHEN (US) — NEEDLE NAILS, ELECTRONICS
ROJIN SHARAFI (IR/AT) — VOCALS, ELECTRONICS
ELVIN BRANDHI (UK) — VOCALS
NIK HUMMER (AT) — MODULAR SYNTHESIZER

videos.title: PULLED BY MAGNETS @ FLUID FORM CLUB #2 | ausland | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Seb Rochford is best known as leader of the post-jazz formation Polar Bear and co-founder of punk jazzers Acoustic Ladyland, as well as a founding member of Sons of Kemet and session drummer for everyone from Pete Doherty to Adele, to Brian Eno. His latest project, Pulled By Magnets, however, sounds nothing like any of the above. It's sludge rock disguised as ambient music; a grindcore album recorded in a cavernous church and layered with subtle spiritual tropes, to the point that it sounds like a piece of sacred minimalism.

Fluid Form Club presents passionately unpredictable music that escapes classification, for a community of curious minds.
Funded by Musikfonds e.V. by means of the Federal Government Commissioner for Culture and the Media

PULLED BY MAGNETS - live @ FLUID FORM CLUB #2 | ausland, Berlin | 2023
